headlines.text: US-Iran ceasefire already shaking: Israel bombs Lebanon killing 250+, Hormuz status murky
Trump warns all US troops stay in Mideast until a 'REAL AGREEMENT' is honored
Iran accuses US of violating ceasefire on day one; Parliament speaker calls talks 'unreasonable'
House Democrats try war powers resolution to curb Trump's Iran authority at pro forma session
Oil whipsaws: WTI back near $100 as Hormuz closure fears return after brief ceasefire euphoria
Democrats romp in Wisconsin Supreme Court race, continue post-2024 overperformance streak
Former AG Pam Bondi refuses to appear before Congress for April 14 Epstein files deposition
Trump threatens to pull out of NATO after meeting with Secretary General Mark Rutte
Gas prices hit $4.12 national average, up over $1 since US-Israel struck Iran in February
Shiloh Jolie-Pitt appears in K-pop star Dayoung music video in quietly cast role

breaking.text: Iran ceasefire in doubt: Hormuz tanker traffic halted again after Israel's Lebanon assault; VP Vance heads to Islamabad for talks Saturday

stories.hed: US-Iran Ceasefire Cracks on Day One as Israel Bombs Lebanon and Hormuz Closes Again
stories.dek: The ink was barely dry on the two-week ceasefire before Israel launched its largest Lebanon attack since Operation Roaring Lion began, killing over 250. Iran responded by halting tanker traffic through Hormuz again, which is either a bold negotiating tactic or proof that 'ceasefire' is doing a lot of heavy lifting as a word.

stories.hed: Lebanon Declares Day of National Mourning After Wave of Israeli Strikes Kills 200+
stories.dek: Israel's Air Force confirmed its largest Lebanon attack since the war began, with Lebanon's health ministry reporting hundreds killed or wounded. The US and Israel maintain Lebanon is not covered by the Iran ceasefire, a position that is testing the limits of the word 'ceasefire.'

stories.hed: Democrats Push War Powers Resolution as Jamie Raskin Briefs Members on 25th Amendment
stories.dek: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries called the two-week ceasefire 'woefully insufficient' and moved to force a war powers vote at today's pro forma session. Meanwhile Rep. Jamie Raskin will brief members on the 25th Amendment Friday, which is either routine civics education or something else entirely.

stories.hed: Trump Rips NATO Allies as 'Very Disappointing' After Secretary General Rutte Visit
stories.dek: Following a White House meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, Trump threatened to pull out of the alliance and blasted allies for not helping with Iran. Rutte argued Europe had in fact helped, which apparently was not the correct answer.

stories.hed: Iran's Parliament Says the US Already Violated Three of Its Ten Ceasefire Conditions Before Talks Even Start
stories.dek: Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf declared negotiations 'unreasonable' because the US allegedly broke three clauses of Tehran's 10-point plan before the first round of talks in Islamabad this weekend. At this rate the Islamabad talks may spend most of their time debating what the ceasefire said rather than what comes next.

markets.context: Stocks held narrow gains mid-session as ceasefire optimism battled oil's resurgence above $99, with WTI spiking over 5% on renewed Hormuz closure fears after Israel's Lebanon strikes.

gas.note: Gas is up over a dollar since February — turns out starting a war near 20% of the world's oil supply has consequences at the pump.

popCulture.hed: Shiloh Jolie-Pitt Secretly Stars in K-Pop Music Video for Dayoung's 'What's a Girl to Do'
popCulture.dek: Brad and Angelina's 19-year-old daughter Shiloh was quietly cast in K-pop star Dayoung's latest music video, sending the internet into a full spiral. It's the celebrity offspring crossover event nobody saw coming but everyone is absolutely losing their minds over.

genZ.definition: A real European truck robbery in which over 400,000 KitKat bars — roughly 12 tons of chocolate — were stolen in transit, instantly becoming the internet's favorite crime story of the month. The sheer absurdity of a large-scale chocolate heist triggered an avalanche of memes calling it the 'chocolatiest crime in history' across TikTok and Instagram this week.
genZ.usage: The Iran ceasefire lasted about as long as those KitKat bars survived near a hungry thief — gone before anyone could enjoy it.

tldr: Day 41 of the Iran war brought a ceasefire that began falling apart within hours, as Israel hammered Lebanon, Iran briefly re-closed the Strait of Hormuz, and oil prices snapped back above $99 while confused diplomats booked flights to Islamabad. At home, Democrats pushed a war powers vote, gas hit $4.12 a gallon, and the internet was mostly focused on a chocolate heist.
